data.title: As a new project member, I see a plain-language explanation of each file before it is written so I understand what is being configured
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add 1-2 sentence plain-language explanations before each infrastructure file write during onboarding
**Why**: Non-technical users currently see files being written without understanding what they are or why they exist — especially the shim
**Scope**:
- IN: Explanations for dso-config.conf (workflow settings), .claude/scripts/dso shim (command-line shortcut to plugin scripts), pre-commit hooks (automated quality checks), and other infrastructure files written during Phase 3
- OUT: Explanations for files written by sub-skills invoked after onboarding (e.g., architect-foundation)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a 1-2 sentence plain-language explanation is displayed before writing each infrastructure file, covering at minimum dso-config.conf, the shim, and pre-commit hooks
  ← Satisfies: "Before writing each infrastructure file, the skill displays a 1-2 sentence plain-language explanation"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Explanation text is new LLM instruction content — verify it appears in output via behavioral testing

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
